name: Story Building Workflow
summary: How stories are built: passage_briefs (structure) → passages (prose). Use story_spark for briefs, scene_weave for prose.

definitions.meaning: Structural planning document that defines a passage's purpose, beats, and choices BEFORE prose is written.
distinguishing_features: Created by Plotwright via story_spark playbook
Contains: title, summary, beats, choices with targets (pids)
Must exist BEFORE a passage can be written

definitions.meaning: Full prose content that players read. Dialogue, sensory detail, and formatted choices.
distinguishing_features: Created by Scene Smith via scene_weave playbook
Derived from a passage_brief (requires brief to exist first)
Contains: prose, formatted choices, pid links

obligations.when: User requests passages or a story

obligations.must: Create passage_briefs for all planned passages
Create passages from existing passage_briefs only
Validate briefs (pre-gate) before scene_weave, validate passages (full-gate) after

rules.statement: To get N passages, you MUST first create N passage_briefs via story_spark
rules.reasoning: Scene Smith can only write prose from existing briefs. No brief = no passage.

rules.statement: story_spark creates structure (briefs), scene_weave creates prose (passages)
rules.reasoning: Structure first, prose second. Don't skip to prose without planning.

rules.statement: If user asks for 'a story with 10 passages', interpret as: run story_spark for 10 briefs, then scene_weave for 10 passages
rules.reasoning: Common request pattern. Briefs come first, always.

procedures.goal: Create a story with N passages
steps: Clarify requirements with user if needed (genre, tone, themes)
Run story_spark playbook: Plotwright creates N passage_briefs
Run scene_weave playbook: Scene Smith writes prose for each brief
preconditions: User has specified desired number of passages

procedures.goal: Add more passages to existing story
steps: Search workspace for existing passage_briefs to understand current structure
Run story_spark to create new passage_briefs (Plotwright)
Run scene_weave to create new passages (Scene Smith)
postconditions: New passages integrated into existing story

heuristics.context: User says 'create 3 passages' or 'a story with 3 passages'
heuristics.guidance: This means: 3 passage_briefs (via story_spark) then 3 passages (via scene_weave)
examples: situation: 'create a mystery with 5 passages' | approach: story_spark for 5 briefs → scene_weave for 5 passages
situation: 'expand to 10 passages' | approach: story_spark for additional briefs → scene_weave for those passages

heuristics.guidance: Structure questions → story_spark. Prose questions → scene_weave.
examples: need: 'write prose for existing briefs' | playbook: scene_weave
need: 'add new branch point' | playbook: story_spark (structure change)

warnings.failure_mode: Delegating to Scene Smith without existing passage_briefs
warnings.consequence: Scene Smith has no structure to write from. Will fail or hallucinate structure.
warnings.prevention: Always run story_spark first to create briefs, then scene_weave for prose.

warnings.failure_mode: Interpreting 'passages' request as single passage_brief with internal passages
warnings.consequence: Creates 1 brief with 3 sub-components instead of 3 separate briefs → 3 passages.
warnings.prevention: 'N passages' means N separate artifacts. One brief = one passage.

warnings.failure_mode: Skipping story_spark and going directly to prose
warnings.consequence: No structural planning. Inconsistent branching. Quality issues.
User asked for passages but only got briefs. Incomplete delivery.
warnings.prevention: If user wants 'passages' (prose), must complete scene_weave after story_spark.
